name: GR 92: Vilanova i la Geltrú Coastal Loop
name_original: Desde Sitges a Vilanova — circular por la costa
description: Hike the 7.2-mile circular coastal path from Sitges to Vilanova, offering moderate difficulty and stunning Mediterranean views.
region_intro: Hiking around Sant Pere De Ribes offers diverse natural landscapes, from the rugged limestone formations of Garraf Natural Park to serene vineyards and rolling hills. The region features distinctive geological elements, including hidden valleys and chasms, alongside aromatic pine forests. While primarily inland, Sant Pere De Ribes also provides access to coastal paths with Mediterranean views. Montgròs peak, at 358 meters, is the highest point within the municipality, offering panoramic vistas. Best hiking trails around Sant Pere De Ribes The most popular hiking route is GR 92: Vilanova i la Geltrú Coastal Loop, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 6 minutes to complete. This moderate coastal path offers views of the Mediterranean Sea. Another top favourite among local hikers is Coll de l'Entreforc y Sitges — circular desde Sant Pere de Ribes, a difficult 12.1 miles (19.4 km) path. This challenging circular route starts from Sant Pere de Ribes and requires good fitness. Local hikers also love the From Sant Pere de Ribes to the Buddhist Monastery — circular, a 15.2 miles (24.5 km) trail leading through Garraf Natural Park, often completed in about 7 hours. Hiking around Sant Pere De Ribes is defined by limestone formations, extensive vineyards, and coastal paths. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more demanding climbs. The routes in Sant Pere De Ribes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Sant Pere De Ribes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Sant Pere De Ribes?
A: There are over 100 hiking trails in and around Sant Pere De Ribes, offering a wide variety of options for all fitness levels. You'll find everything from easy strolls through vineyards to more challenging routes in the Garraf Natural Park.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on hikes around Sant Pere De Ribes?
A: The region offers a diverse mix of landscapes. You can explore the rugged limestone formations, hidden valleys, and aromatic pine forests of the Garraf Natural Park. There are also serene routes through extensive vineyards and rolling hills, and even coastal paths providing stunning Mediterranean views.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Sant Pere De Ribes offers many easy trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1469930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Vineyards and Hills loop from Sant Pere de Ribes&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option that provides scenic views without significant elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes in Sant Pere De Ribes?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more demanding experience, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353889056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de l'Entreforc y Sitges — circular desde Sant Pere de Ribes&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation changes and longer distances, requiring good fitness. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353878142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Sant Pere de Ribes to the Buddhist Monastery — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the Garraf Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Sant Pere De Ribes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353889056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de l'Entreforc y Sitges — circular desde Sant Pere de Ribes&lt;/a&gt; and the easier &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1469930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Vineyards and Hills loop from Sant Pere de Ribes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting natural features or landmarks can I explore while hiking?
A: While hiking, you can discover unique geological features like caves and sinkholes within the Garraf Natural Park. The area is also home to several historical sites. You might encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Muntanyeta&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/440250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet Castle and the Foix Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The Buddhist Monastery, located within the Garraf Natural Park, is also a notable point of interest.

Q: Are there hikes with good viewpoints in Sant Pere De Ribes?
A: Yes, the region is known for its panoramic views. The Montgròs peak, at 358 meters, is the highest point within the municipality and offers spectacular vistas of Ribes and the coastline. Many trails, including the Sant Pere de Ribes – Montgrós Route, lead to scenic viewpoints across the Garraf region.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Sant Pere De Ribes?
A: The hiking trails around Sant Pere De Ribes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from coastal paths to rugged park trails, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various ability levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sant Pere De Ribes?
A: The Mediterranean climate makes Sant Pere De Ribes suitable for hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for longer treks. Even in winter, the mild climate can be inviting, though some trails might be cooler. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Sant Pere De Ribes dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in the Garraf Natural Park and surrounding areas are suitable for dogs, especially those that are less crowded. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to carry enough water for both of you. Check specific trail regulations if you plan to visit a nature reserve.

Q: Is it possible to reach hiking trails using public transport?
A: While some trailheads might be accessible by local bus services, particularly those closer to towns like Sitges or Vilanova i la Geltrú, many routes within the Garraf Natural Park are best reached by car. It's recommended to check local public transport schedules and routes in advance if you plan to rely on it.

Q: Are there options for parking near the trailheads?
A: Yes, for many popular hiking areas and trailheads around Sant Pere De Ribes, you will find designated parking areas. However, during peak seasons or on weekends, these can fill up quickly, especially for trails within the Garraf Natural Park. Arriving early is often a good strategy to secure a spot.
tips: Ruined building of the old Atlantida nightclub closed in 2013 by the new Costa law after 30 years. Very decadent and picturesque atmosphere but that gives a feeling of nostalgia for the times that were and are no more.

Very beautiful and adventurous path from Sitges to Vilanova i Geltru.
Attention: The path often leads very close to the railway tracks (no fence).

Vilanova i la Geltrù Lighthouse, now houses the Vilanova Sea Museum. The museum is cheap and also on some days it is free. You can walk outside and see the boats outside for free. Two steps from Sant Cristofol Beach and at the beginning (or end) of the path that connects Sitges and Vilanova.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this coastal hike?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour for the circular route from Sitges to Vilanova i la Geltrú is estimated to take approximately 3 hours and 6 minutes to complete. If you choose to hike one-way, the path typically takes between 2.5 to 3 hours.

faqs.question: Is this hike suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this coastal path is generally considered to have a low to moderate difficulty level, making it suitable for a wide range of ages and fitness levels, including families. While some sources classify it as moderate, it's largely flat with little gradient, making it accessible for inexperienced walkers.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like and what footwear is recommended?
faqs.answer: The path features varied terrain, including tarmac, dirt paths, rocky sections, and sand. You'll encounter areas with pebbles and stones. Given this, sturdy footwear is highly recommended over flip-flops to ensure comfort and stability.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and scenic views along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy stunning panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea, dramatic cliffs, and capes. Highlights include the Ruins of Club La Atlántida, the Coastal Railway Trail from Sitges to Vilanova i la Geltrú, and the Saint Christopher Lighthouse. The route also passes through the Paratge Natural dels Colls i Miralpeix, offering views of secluded coves like Cala Xica and Platja de l'Home Mort.

faqs.question: How do I get to the trailhead and where can I park?
faqs.answer: Both Sitges and Vilanova i la Geltrú have train stations, making it easy to access the trail by public transport, especially if you plan a one-way hike. For parking, you would typically look for public parking options available in Sitges or Vilanova, depending on your starting point. Specific parking details are not provided, but both towns are well-equipped for visitors.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the coastal walk with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in populated areas, near railway tracks, and to respect local regulations regarding pets on beaches or in protected natural areas.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this coastal path?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring is particularly recommended to avoid the summer crowds and enjoy pleasant temperatures for hiking and swimming in the coves. Sunset views from Sitges are especially spectacular, bathing the town and sea in golden hues.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Garraf Massif?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this coastal trail. The route passes through the Paratge Natural dels Colls i Miralpeix, which is part of the Garraf Massif, but it is generally accessible to the public without special permits or fees for hiking.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other long-distance paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route forms a significant section of the extensive GR-92 Coastal Path, a long-distance footpath that stretches along the Mediterranean coast. It also covers parts of other official trails such as Catalunya E22 and Camí de Vilanova a Sitges.

faqs.question: Can I swim in the coves along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The trail offers numerous opportunities to discover secluded coves, such as Cala Xica, Cala dels Gegants, and Platja de l'Home Mort. These spots are perfect for a refreshing swim in transparent, intensely blue waters, especially during warmer months. Remember to bring appropriate gear if you plan to take a dip.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route, and can I do it one-way?
faqs.answer: While the Komoot tour is mapped as a circular route, the coastal path itself is often hiked one-way between Sitges and Vilanova i la Geltrú. The convenience of train stations in both towns makes it easy to complete the walk in one direction and return by train, offering flexibility for your day trip.

waypoints.name: Coastal Railway Trail from Sitges to Vilanova i la Geltrú
